---
title: "5.1.5 Retrieval and location of web pages"
subtitle: "The internet and the World Wide Web · P183-P184"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.34em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## HTML and Web Servers

### Where web pages are stored

<span class="term">HTML</span> is a language used to display content on browsers.

All websites are written in HTML and hosted on a <span class="term">web server</span> that has its own <span class="term">IP address</span>.

To retrieve pages from a website your browser needs to know this IP address.

## DNS

### Finding IP addresses

The <span class="term">Domain Name Server (DNS)</span>, also known as domain name system, is a system for finding IP addresses for a domain name given in a URL.

URLs and domain name servers eliminate the need for a user to memorise IP addresses.

## DNS Conversion

### URL to IP address

The DNS process involves converting a URL into an IP address the computer can understand.

Example:

::: {.clean-table}
| URL | IP address |
|---|---|
| `www.hoddereducation.co.uk` | `107.162.140.19` |
:::

The DNS process involves <span class="mark">more than one server</span>.

## Figure 5.3

### DNS is used to locate and retrieve a web page

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.3 How DNS is used to locate and retrieve a web page screenshot placeholder
:::

## Retrieval Sequence

### Steps 1-3

::: {.tight-list}
1. The user opens their browser and types in the URL; the browser asks DNS server (1) for the website IP address.
2. If DNS server (1) cannot find the URL in its database or cache, it sends a request to DNS server (2).
3. DNS server (2) finds the URL and maps it to the IP address; this IP address is sent back to DNS server (1), which stores it in its cache/database.
:::

## Retrieval Sequence

### Steps 4-5

::: {.tight-list}
4. The IP address is sent back to the user's computer.
5. The computer sets up communication with the website server and the required pages are downloaded.
:::

HTML files are sent from the website server to the computer. The browser interprets the HTML and displays the information on the user's computer.

## Classroom Check

### DNS and retrieval

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- websites are written in HTML and hosted on a web server with an IP address
- DNS finds IP addresses for domain names given in URLs
- DNS avoids the need for users to memorise IP addresses
- DNS may use more than one server and can use cache/database records
- after the IP address is returned, HTML files are downloaded and interpreted by the browser
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Cookies

The next deck explains how websites store small files or code on a user's computer.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
